calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-20-2015 Off Calendar

Defendant Netfortris Acquisition Co., Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs Complaint Ccp Sections 418.10 And 410.30
Off Calendar Jul-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
William C. Wilka, of Dudnick Detwiler Rivin & Stikker LLP, Tel. 415.982.1400, for plaintiffs;
Justine N. Margolis, of Dentons US LLP, Tel. 415.882.0168, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT NETFORTRIS ACQUISITION CO., INC.'S Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs Complaint CCP Sections 418.10 And 410.30 is granted. The complaint is dismissed pursuant to CCP secs. 418.10 and 410.30. (See Olinick v. BMG Entertainment (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 1286, 1294, "The procedure for enforcing a forum selection clause is a motion to stay or dismiss for forum non conveniens.") Plaintiffs IXC Holdings, Inc., IXC, Inc., and NW, Inc. entered into an Asset Purchase Agreement with Defendant Netfortris Acquisition Co., Inc., which contains a broad forum selection clause that provides that "[t]he parties hereto hereby irrevocably submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of any federal or state court located within the City and State of New York over any dispute out of or relating to this Agreement or any of the transactions contemplated hereby and each party hereby irrevocably agrees that all claims in respect of such dispute or any suit, action, proceeding related thereto may be heard and determined in such courts." (Declaration of Grant, Ex. A, Asset Purchase Agreement, sec. 11.3.) The same parties subsequently entered into a Settlement Agreement that does not contain a forum selection clause. (Complaint, Ex. A.) Although the complaint in this action alleges breaches of the Settlement Agreement, the dispute relates to the Asset Purchase Agreement, given that the Settlement Agreement was entered into because each party alleged that the other breached the Asset Purchase Agreement. Accordingly, because the dispute set forth in the complaint relates to the Asset Purchase Agreement, the forum selection clause applies and New York is the only available venue for this dispute. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
